Title: Cotton Leaf Curl Multan virus C4 protein suppresses both transcriptional and posttranscriptional gene silencing by interacting with SAM synthetase

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Geminiviral C4 protein; viral silencing suppressor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected after transient expression and PVX/TYLCCNV-based expression in leaves
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; R13A mutation does not alter localization
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLCuMuV infection; PVX-based expression; TYLCCNV-based expression; agroinfiltration
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mass spectrometry-guided interactor identification; co-IP; GST pull-down; BiFC; luciferase complementation; overexpression in PVX and agroinfiltration; bisulfite sequencing; siRNA blot; mutant virus infection assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses both transcriptional and post-transcriptional gene silencing, reduces host and viral DNA methylation, lowers SAM accumulation through SAMS inhibition, and enhances geminivirus infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Methyl donor / activated amino acid metabolite
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports methylation reactions underlying antiviral defense; C4 reduces this pool indirectly by inhibiting SAMS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4 physically interacts with NbSAMS proteins, especially analyzed in detail with NbSAMS2. 2. C4 inhibits NbSAMS2 enzymatic activity and reduces SAM levels in infected plants. 3. C4 suppresses both TGS and PTGS and thereby promotes viral DNA accumulation and symptom severity.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS for interactor identification; LC-MS/MS for SAM quantification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arg13 is essential for SAMS interaction and suppressor activity. Because C4 overlaps Rep, the R13A mutation also creates RepE65G, but this Rep change did not alter replication in protoplast assays.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Disrupting the C4-SAMS interaction could be a target for engineering geminivirus resistance without directly eliminating host SAMS activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Viral suppressor enhances disease severity and viral accumulation; broad interference with methyl cycle may affect host growth and methylation homeostasis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PVX vector expression; 35S-GFP coexpression assays; Y10mβ-C4 expression system
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Reduces cytosine methylation of 35S-GFP transgene promoter, endogenous NbTnt1, and viral intergenic region via SAMS inhibition

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Mutant form of C4 in CLCuMuV genome EF465535
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Engineered mutant expressed in infection and transient assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected after transient/PVX expression in leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mutant virus infection; transient expression; PVX-based expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutagenesis; co-IP; GST pull-down; BiFC; in vitro enzyme assay; PVX expression; mutant infectious clone assays; bisulfite sequencing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loses interaction with NbSAMS2, fails to inhibit SAMS activity, fails to suppress TGS/PTGS, leads to higher viral DNA methylation and reduced viral accumulation/symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Methyl donor / activated amino acid metabolite
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mutant no longer effectively decreases SAM-linked methylation defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The R13A mutation abolishes C4 interaction with NbSAMS2. 2. C4R13A cannot inhibit NbSAMS2 enzymatic activity or suppress TGS/PTGS. 3. Virus carrying C4R13A shows attenuated infection and increased viral DNA methylation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS for SAM quantification in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: R13A also causes RepE65G because of overlapping ORFs, but RepE65G alone did not impair replication in protoplast complementation assays.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights a precise viral interface residue that could be targeted in resistance engineering or antiviral screening.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PVX vector expression; mutant viral infectious clone
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Associated with enhanced methylation of viral intergenic region relative to wild-type C4 virus

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: S-adenosyl methionine synthetase; methyl cycle enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: SAM production in the methyl cycle
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed SAM-forming step in methyl cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methionine and ATP into S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target metabolite and supplies methyl donor pool for downstream DNA/RNA/protein methylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Methyl donor / activated amino acid metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Universal methyl donor for transmethylation reactions, supporting DNA methylation-associated antiviral defense and gene silencing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaf tissues; highest among cloned NbSAMS homologs based on RNA-seq reads
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in leaf tissues; targeted in systemic/apical leaves in VIGS assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLCuMuV infection; TYLCCNV infection; VIGS silencing; transient expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC-MS/MS identification of interactor; luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; GST pull-down; BiFC; in vitro enzyme assay; VIGS knockdown; LC-MS/MS quantification of SAM; bisulfite sequencing; metabolite and viral DNA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for methylation-dependent silencing in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SAM accumulation and supports methylation-mediated TGS/PTGS; silencing reduces SAMS function, lowers antiviral methylation, and increases geminivirus accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbSAMS2 was identified as a direct interactor of CLCuMuV C4. 2. NbSAMS2 enzymatic activity is inhibited by wild-type C4 but not by C4R13A. 3. Silencing NbSAMS2 reverses both TGS and PTGS, reduces viral DNA methylation, and enhances susceptibility to CLCuMuV and TYLCCNV.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression evidence for NbSAMS homolog expression in leaf tissues; LC-MS/MS protein identification and SAM metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among eight SAMS homologs in the genome, NbSAMS1/2/3 were cloned; NbSAMS2 was emphasized because of highest leaf expression and strong interaction evidence. Silencing sometimes weakly affected NbSAMS1 depending on fragment used.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing SAMS activity could strengthen methylation-based antiviral defense, but strong silencing causes albino/dwarf phenotypes, so engineering would require careful dosage or tissue-specific tuning.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong reduction causes severe developmental defects including albino and dwarf phenotypes; possible effects on pigment synthesis and broader methylation-dependent processes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used in GFP/PTGS assays; native context for endogenous NbSAMS2
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts upstream of DNA methylation capacity by supplying SAM; silencing lowers methylation of viral DNA, transgene promoter, and endogenous loci
